This is a nice little apple tree that overhangs a public sidewalk. In years past, it's had bumper crops that litter the ground with apples. This year (2016), the tree is completely bare. However, this just happens to be a bad year for apples in general. Access

There are three ornamental crab apple trees right on this corner near Lamson Library. The fruits are slightly smaller than one inch each and appear to be untouched by birds or any other foragers. Access: Public.

An apple tree growing in the middle of campus. It's a mature tree that often bears fruit; however I haven't actually gotten a chance to eat it's apples yet. Access: Public.
This crabapple tree overhangs the sidewalk. In years past there have been bumper crops, but this year (2016) there are very few fruit on the tree. Access: Private but overhanging.
